subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand market operations and buyer-seller interactions
Observe price variations and market types
Relate market concepts to daily life and local economy

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Local Market Observation
topics_covered: Observation of local markets or shops
teaching_strategy: Show pictures and videos of local markets
student_activities: Take notes on market layout and shop types

lesson_plan.title: Types of Markets and Comparison
topics_covered: Differences in size, goods, and crowd
teaching_strategy: Show examples via charts or slides
Ask students to compare two markets
student_activities: Fill a comparison table of two markets

lesson_plan.title: Goods and Services Analysis
teaching_strategy: Ask students to sort items into goods and services
student_activities: Create a table of at least 5 goods and 5 services

lesson_plan.title: Price Study and Bargaining
teaching_strategy: Demonstrate price differences via charts or pictures
student_activities: Record prices of 3 items in two shops

lesson_plan.title: Trade and Exchange Systems
topics_covered: Barter system and modern money trade
How trade helps people get what they need
teaching_strategy: Discuss real-life examples of barter vs cash transactions
student_activities: Simulate barter or trade in class
Identify items that can be exchanged or sold

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Discussion, and Mini Project
topics_covered: Recap market types, goods, services, and trade
Discuss observations from visits or role-plays
teaching_strategy: Class discussion connecting markets to local economy
student_activities: Prepare a short report or poster about a local market
Share key findings with the class

teaching_aids: Charts and props for trade demonstration
Drawing sheets and colors for mini project
teacher_tip: Encourage students to relate markets to their own shopping experiences. Use charts, role-plays, and observation activities to develop analytical thinking.
